description: Coding-domain MOLT Need blocks. Need answers: 'What must be true first?' Each block describes a software engineering prerequisite, enabling condition, or required state that must exist before the coding Aim can be achieved.
usage: Select the Need block that describes what must already be in place before the coding Aim can succeed. If the Need is absent, the Aim cannot be reached regardless of effort.

ENVIRONMENT_SETUP.description: What must be true about the development environment before work can begin

blocks.condition: The required language runtime or interpreter must be present and at the correct version before code can run
examples: Node.js must be installed at the required version before npm scripts run
Python must be present in the virtual environment before the app starts
the JVM must be installed before a Java build runs

blocks.condition: All packages listed in the project manifest must be installed before code can import them
examples: npm install must complete before a Node app starts
pip install -r requirements.txt must succeed before running tests
cargo build must resolve all crates before compilation

blocks.condition: All required runtime configuration values must be present in the environment before the application starts
examples: DATABASE_URL must be set before the app can connect to a database
API_SECRET must be injected before auth middleware works
PORT must be defined before the server binds

blocks.condition: Any local dependencies the application requires — databases, message brokers, mock services — must be up before the app starts
examples: a local Postgres instance must be running before the ORM connects
a local Redis must be up before the session store initialises
a mock API server must be running before integration tests use it

blocks.need: Repository cloned and branch checked out
blocks.condition: The source code must be available locally on the correct branch before any development work begins
examples: the repo must be cloned before running the project locally
the feature branch must be checked out before implementing a ticket
the correct tag must be checked out before a hotfix is applied

CODE_AVAILABILITY.description: What must be true about the codebase before it can be worked on

blocks.condition: The existing codebase must compile or type-check cleanly before new work is layered on top
examples: the TypeScript project must compile before new code is added
the Rust project must build before a new module is introduced
the existing test suite must be in a passing state before a refactor begins

blocks.condition: All existing tests must be green before new changes are introduced, so regressions can be detected
examples: the full test suite must pass before a PR is opened
no failing tests must exist before a refactor begins
the regression suite must be green before a release is cut

blocks.condition: The specific file, function, or module to be changed must be located before work begins
examples: the function with the bug must be found before a fix is written
the module to be refactored must be identified before restructuring begins
the endpoint to be optimised must be located before profiling

blocks.condition: The developer must understand how the target code fits into the larger system before making changes
examples: the dependencies of a module must be understood before it is extracted
the callers of a function must be identified before its signature is changed
the data flow through a component must be understood before state is modified

blocks.condition: The git history must be available so regressions can be traced and changes can be understood in context
examples: git log must be available to bisect a regression
blame history must be accessible to understand why a line was written
the full commit history must be present before an ADR references prior decisions

SPECIFICATION_CLARITY.description: What must be true about requirements before implementation begins

blocks.condition: The conditions that determine whether an implementation is correct must be stated before coding begins
examples: user story acceptance criteria must be written before a feature is implemented
the expected return values of a function must be specified before it is written
the API contract must be agreed before the handler is implemented

blocks.condition: What is in and out of scope for the current task must be clear before work starts
examples: the boundary of a refactor must be agreed before touching code
which edge cases are in scope must be clear before writing tests
whether backwards compatibility is required must be decided before changing an API

blocks.condition: The API, function signature, or data contract must be specified before implementation begins
examples: the REST endpoint shape must be agreed before the handler is written
the function signature must be decided before the body is implemented
the event schema must be finalised before the producer and consumer are coded

blocks.condition: Known boundary conditions and unexpected inputs must be listed before implementation begins so they are addressed
examples: null input handling must be specified before the function is written
empty array behaviour must be defined before a list-processing function is coded
overflow handling must be decided before arithmetic code is written

blocks.condition: How the code should behave when things go wrong must be defined before happy-path implementation begins
examples: what to return on a database connection failure must be specified before a repository is written
whether to throw or return an error must be decided before a parser is coded
which HTTP status codes map to which failure modes must be agreed before a controller is implemented

TEST_INFRASTRUCTURE.description: What must be true before tests can be written or run

blocks.condition: The testing library and runner must be present and configured before tests can be authored or executed
examples: Jest must be installed and configured before unit tests are written
pytest must be present before Python tests run
the test runner must be wired into the CI pipeline before tests are required to pass

blocks.condition: Tests must run in an environment that does not affect or depend on production state
examples: tests must use a separate test database before they mutate data
tests must mock external APIs before they run in CI without network access
test fixtures must be isolated before tests run in parallel safely

blocks.condition: Test doubles for external dependencies must exist before tests that rely on them can be written
examples: a mock for the payment gateway must be created before checkout tests can run
database fixtures must be seeded before integration tests can run
a fake file system must be set up before file-handling tests can be written

blocks.condition: For TDD and bug fixes, a test that demonstrates the current failure must exist before the fix or feature is implemented
examples: a failing test that reproduces the bug must be written before the patch
a failing acceptance test must exist before the feature is built
a failing property test must be present before the algorithm is corrected

blocks.condition: The current test coverage level must be measured and recorded before it is used as a comparison point
examples: current coverage percentage must be recorded before a coverage improvement sprint
a coverage report must exist before CI enforces a minimum threshold
coverage baseline must be stored before a refactor claims it did not reduce coverage

BUILD_PIPELINE.description: What must be true about the build system before code can be compiled or packaged

blocks.condition: The build system must be set up and producing a correct output before development relies on it
examples: Webpack config must be correct before the bundle is trusted
the Makefile must be present and correct before make commands are used
the Gradle build must be configured before the project compiles

blocks.condition: The project must build without warnings or errors before the output is deployed or used
examples: TypeScript must compile with zero errors before a release is cut
the Docker image must build successfully before it is pushed to a registry
the binary must link without warnings before it is used in a test

blocks.condition: The same source code must always produce the same artifact regardless of when or where it is built
examples: lockfile must be committed before reproducible installs are guaranteed
base Docker image must be pinned before builds are reproducible
build timestamps must be excluded before binary reproducibility is verified

blocks.condition: For performance-sensitive workflows, the cache must be populated before build times are acceptable
examples: Docker layer cache must be primed before CI build times are reasonable
npm cache must be populated before a fast install is expected
Gradle build cache must be warm before incremental builds are fast

blocks.condition: The registry where build outputs are pushed must be reachable before a build can publish its artifact
examples: Docker Hub must be reachable before an image is pushed
npm registry must be accessible before a package is published
the internal artifact repository must be reachable before a CI deploy pushes a build

DEBUGGING_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before a bug can be diagnosed and fixed

blocks.condition: The failure must be triggerable reliably before a fix can be written
examples: a minimal reproduction case must exist before debugging begins
the exact input that causes the crash must be known before tracing
the sequence of steps that triggers the bug must be documented before fixing

blocks.condition: Observable evidence of the failure must be present before root cause analysis begins
examples: the stack trace must be available before the crash is diagnosed
the error log must be captured before the failure location is determined
the failing request payload must be recorded before the input is examined

blocks.condition: The build must include debug information before a debugger can be attached or symbols resolved
examples: the binary must be built with debug symbols before gdb can show line numbers
source maps must be generated before a browser debugger shows TypeScript lines
the app must be running in debug mode before a breakpoint can be set

blocks.condition: Which users, data, or systems are affected must be established before the severity and fix priority are determined
examples: the number of affected requests must be known before escalation level is set
the affected data range must be understood before a remediation plan is written
the affected services must be identified before the fix is scoped

blocks.condition: A test that captures the bug must exist before the fix is applied, so it can be verified as resolved and guarded against return
examples: a failing unit test must be written before the bug is patched
a failing integration test must exist before the service interaction is fixed
a property test that exposes the edge case must be authored before the algorithm is corrected

DEPENDENCY_STATE.description: What must be true about third-party code before it can be used

blocks.condition: The version of a dependency being used must be compatible with the project's runtime and other packages
examples: peer dependency requirements must be met before a package is installed
the library version must support the Node.js version in use
the package must be compatible with the framework version before it is added

blocks.condition: A dependency must be free of known critical security vulnerabilities before it is used in production
examples: a CVE audit must pass before a package is added to a production build
a vulnerable transitive dependency must be resolved before a release is cut
npm audit must report no critical issues before deployment

blocks.condition: The licence of a dependency must be compatible with the project's distribution model before it is added
examples: a GPL licence must be confirmed as acceptable before adding it to a proprietary project
all licences must be reviewed before open-sourcing an internal tool
licence approval must be obtained before a package is added to a commercial product

blocks.condition: The dependency lockfile must be checked into version control before builds are reproducible across environments
examples: package-lock.json must be committed before CI installs match local
Cargo.lock must be in the repo before build outputs are consistent
poetry.lock must be committed before the Python environment is reproducible

blocks.condition: The interface and behaviour of the dependency must be understood before it is used, to avoid misuse or surprise
examples: the library's error throwing behaviour must be understood before it is wrapped
pagination semantics of an SDK must be read before a list API is called
the rate limits of a client library must be known before it is used in a high-volume context

SCHEMA_DATA_MODEL.description: What must be true about data structures before code operates on them

blocks.condition: The data structure must be specified and not subject to imminent change before code is written against it
examples: the database schema must be agreed before ORM models are written
the API response schema must be finalised before a client is coded
the event format must be stable before consumer code is written

blocks.condition: All pending database migrations must be run before the application code that depends on the new schema is executed
examples: the column must be added before the ORM field can be used
the table rename migration must be applied before queries using the new name are run
the index must be created before the query that relies on it is expected to be fast

blocks.condition: Representative data must exist in the test database before tests that query or modify it can run
examples: user fixtures must be seeded before authentication tests run
product records must exist before cart tests can add items
baseline data must be present before a migration test verifies transformation

blocks.condition: The types and nullability of all fields must be decided before code that reads or writes them is authored
examples: whether a timestamp is nullable must be agreed before the ORM field is defined
whether an ID is a string or integer must be decided before it is used in a route
the precision of a monetary amount must be specified before arithmetic on it is implemented

blocks.condition: The rules that determine whether input data is acceptable must be defined before the validation layer is coded
examples: email format requirements must be specified before a validator is written
password strength rules must be agreed before a password input is validated
required vs. optional fields must be listed before a schema validator is configured

ACCESS_AND_PERMISSIONS.description: What must be true about access control before protected resources can be used

blocks.condition: Credentials for external services must exist and be injected before any code that calls those services can run
examples: an API key for a mapping service must be issued before the integration is testable
an OAuth token must be generated before authenticated API calls succeed
a service account credential must be provisioned before a cloud SDK authenticates

blocks.condition: The developer must have read and write access to the relevant repository before contributing
examples: push access to the repo must be granted before a PR can be opened
read access must exist before the codebase can be cloned
the developer must be added to the GitHub org before CI secrets are accessible

blocks.condition: The developer must have access to the required environment before they can test, debug, or deploy
examples: SSH access to a staging server must be granted before remote debugging
AWS IAM permissions must be in place before Terraform applies
Kubernetes RBAC must allow the developer to read pod logs before debugging a deployment

blocks.condition: A connection to the required database must be possible before any code that reads or writes data can run
examples: database credentials must be correct before ORM queries succeed
network access to the DB must be open before a connection can be established
the database must be reachable from the test environment before integration tests pass

blocks.condition: Credentials must be stable and not mid-rotation before code that depends on them is run or deployed
examples: a key rotation must be complete before dependent services are restarted
a cert renewal must be done before the old cert expires and services break
API key rotation must be complete and propagated before CI runs that use the new key

CI_CD_STATE.description: What must be true about the CI/CD pipeline before code can be merged or deployed

blocks.condition: All automated checks in the CI pipeline must be green before a PR can be merged
examples: tests must pass before a PR is mergeable
lint and type checks must be clean before merge is allowed
security scan must report no critical issues before CI approves the build

blocks.condition: At least one qualified reviewer must have approved the changes before they are merged
examples: a required reviewer must approve before the merge button is enabled
a security-sensitive change must have a security team approval
an architectural change must be approved by a tech lead

blocks.condition: The environment being deployed to must be in a stable, healthy state before a new release is pushed
examples: staging must not be in a failed state before a new build is deployed to it
the Kubernetes cluster must be healthy before a rollout is triggered
no active incident must be occurring in production before a routine deploy proceeds

blocks.condition: No other deployment must be running before a new one is started, to avoid conflicts
examples: a previous rollout must complete or be rolled back before a new one starts
a database migration must finish before an application deploy begins
a colleague's deployment must succeed or fail before the next deploy proceeds

blocks.condition: The rollback procedure must be verified in a non-production environment before a risky deploy proceeds
examples: a database migration rollback must be tested in staging before applying to prod
the previous Docker tag must be confirmed as runnable before a risky deploy
the feature flag off-switch must be tested before a complex feature is released

ARCHITECTURE_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about the system design before implementation proceeds

blocks.condition: The technical approach must be documented and reviewed before implementation begins
examples: an ADR must be written and approved before a new architectural pattern is adopted
a design doc must be reviewed before a new service is built
an RFC must be accepted before a breaking API change is implemented

blocks.condition: The responsibilities and interfaces of each component must be agreed before individual services are coded
examples: which service owns user data must be decided before the user service is built
the boundary between two services must be agreed before either is implemented
the shared library API must be defined before the library is coded

blocks.condition: Which service or module is the system of record for each piece of data must be agreed before multi-service code is written
examples: the service that owns the user profile must be identified before others read from it
the system of record for an order must be decided before order events are emitted
which database owns a shared entity must be resolved before foreign keys or API calls are coded

blocks.condition: How to avoid breaking existing consumers when changing an interface must be decided before the change is coded
examples: the deprecation strategy for an old field must be agreed before a schema change
the versioning strategy must be chosen before a new API version is built
the migration path for existing data must be planned before a format change is implemented

blocks.condition: Performance, reliability, and scalability targets must be stated before an implementation that must meet them is designed
examples: the target p99 latency must be set before performance-sensitive code is architected
the availability SLA must be agreed before fault tolerance patterns are chosen
the expected peak load must be known before the scaling strategy is designed

SECURITY_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about security posture before code touches sensitive data or systems

blocks.condition: The security risks relevant to the change must be understood before security controls are designed or implemented
examples: the attack surface must be documented before auth code is written
the trust boundaries must be identified before input validation is designed
the sensitive data flows must be mapped before encryption is applied

blocks.condition: How users will authenticate and how permissions will be checked must be agreed before protected endpoints are coded
examples: the token format must be decided before auth middleware is written
the permission model must be agreed before access control checks are implemented
the session management strategy must be chosen before login code is written

blocks.condition: All data fields that require encryption, masking, or special handling must be identified before the data layer is coded
examples: PII fields must be listed before encryption-at-rest is applied
payment card fields must be identified before PCI scope is defined
password fields must be identified before hashing strategy is applied

blocks.condition: A static analysis scan must have been run and its findings understood before new security-sensitive code is added
examples: an initial SAST scan must run before security improvements are measured
the existing findings must be triaged before new ones are introduced
a clean SAST baseline must exist before security gate enforcement begins

blocks.condition: A vulnerability scan of all dependencies must return no critical or high findings before production code is shipped
examples: npm audit must show no critical issues before a release
OWASP dependency check must pass before a Java release
safety check on Python packages must be clean before deployment

OBSERVABILITY_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about instrumentation before a system can be understood in production

blocks.condition: A logging library and output format must be set up before meaningful logs are emitted
examples: a structured logging library must be initialised before log fields are added
log level configuration must be in place before verbosity is controllable
log output must be routed to the expected destination before logs appear in dashboards

blocks.condition: A metrics scrape endpoint or push mechanism must be active before a monitoring system can collect data
examples: a /metrics endpoint must be exposed before Prometheus can scrape
a StatsD emitter must be configured before metrics reach Datadog
OpenTelemetry instrumentation must be initialised before traces are collected

blocks.condition: Alert conditions and notification routes must be configured before an on-call engineer can be paged
examples: a Prometheus alert rule must exist before PagerDuty receives an incident
a threshold must be defined before an anomaly can trigger a notification
an alert routing policy must be set before alerts reach the right team

blocks.condition: Trace IDs must be passed across service boundaries before distributed traces can be correlated
examples: a trace context header must be forwarded before a full request trace is visible
W3C trace context must be injected before Jaeger spans link correctly
correlation IDs must be carried in log fields before cross-service log correlation works

blocks.condition: A service level objective must be documented before compliance with it can be measured or reported
examples: the error rate SLO must be stated before an error budget is calculated
the latency SLO must be published before a burn rate alert is configured
the availability SLO must be agreed before an SLA is signed with a customer

CONCURRENCY_SAFETY.description: What must be true about shared state and synchronisation before concurrent code is safe

blocks.condition: All data that will be accessed by multiple goroutines, threads, or async tasks must be identified before concurrent access patterns are designed
examples: the shared cache must be identified before a mutex is applied
global counters must be listed before atomic operations are introduced
shared request context must be identified before it is made thread-safe

blocks.condition: The approach to protecting shared state must be decided before concurrent code is written
examples: whether to use a mutex or a channel must be decided before goroutine logic is coded
optimistic vs. pessimistic locking must be chosen before the database transaction is written
actor vs. shared memory must be decided before a concurrent data structure is implemented

blocks.condition: Tests that expose unsafe concurrent access must exist before concurrent code is considered correct
examples: a test with -race flag enabled must exist before a concurrent Go function is shipped
a stress test with many parallel requests must run before a concurrent cache is trusted
a concurrent write test must pass before a shared data structure is used in production

blocks.condition: The lock acquisition order must be reviewed for potential deadlocks before multi-lock code is written
examples: the lock order must be documented before two mutexes are acquired together
the cycle analysis must show no circular waits before a lock hierarchy is deployed
a deadlock detector must have run without findings before multi-threaded code ships

blocks.condition: Operations that may be retried must be verified as safe to run multiple times before they are used in retry-capable systems
examples: a payment handler must be idempotent before it is used with a retry queue
a database write must be idempotent before at-least-once message delivery is used
an event handler must be idempotent before it processes from a replayable log

REFACTORING_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before code can be safely restructured

blocks.condition: The code to be refactored must have enough tests to verify that behavior is preserved throughout the change
examples: unit test coverage must be adequate before a function is extracted
integration tests must cover the module before it is restructured
the critical path must be covered by tests before a refactor begins

blocks.condition: What the code is supposed to do must be captured before it is restructured, so correctness can be verified after
examples: a docstring or comment describing the intended behavior must exist before refactoring
acceptance tests that define the expected outputs must pass before the refactor begins
the existing behavior must be characterised with golden tests before it is changed

blocks.condition: Every place that calls the function or module being changed must be located before the refactor begins
examples: all usages of a function must be found before its signature is changed
all importers of a module must be identified before it is renamed or moved
all callers of an API must be mapped before a breaking change is introduced

blocks.condition: The boundaries of the change must be defined so the refactor does not grow uncontrollably
examples: the set of files to be changed must be agreed before work begins
whether to rename across the repo or just within a package must be decided upfront
the depth of restructuring must be bounded before a long refactor session starts

blocks.need: No in-progress work depends on target code
blocks.condition: No other work-in-progress branch should be actively modifying the code being refactored, to avoid expensive merge conflicts
examples: all feature branches touching the target module must be merged or coordinated before the refactor
the team must be aware of the refactor before starting work on related code
no other PR must have an open change to the file being restructured

MIGRATION_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before a migration can be applied safely

blocks.condition: A migration must have run successfully in a non-production environment before it is applied to production
examples: a database migration must succeed in staging before running in prod
an infrastructure change must be validated in a test environment first
a data transformation migration must be verified on a copy of production data

blocks.condition: A script or procedure to reverse the migration must exist and be tested before the forward migration runs
examples: a down migration must exist before the up migration is applied to production
a Terraform destroy plan must be reviewed before apply
a data restoration procedure must be tested before a destructive migration

blocks.condition: A current backup of the data or system being migrated must exist before the migration begins
examples: a database backup must be taken before a schema migration runs
a snapshot must exist before an infrastructure migration
a data export must be saved before a destructive backfill is applied

blocks.condition: The approach for applying the migration without service interruption must be validated before the migration runs on a live system
examples: the online schema change tool must be verified before a large table migration
the expand-contract pattern must be confirmed before a field rename
a canary deploy path must be tested before a breaking API migration

blocks.condition: All downstream consumers of the data or API being migrated must be confirmed as compatible with the new format before migration
examples: all consumers of an event schema must handle the new format before the producer changes
all API clients must support the new response shape before v1 is deprecated
all downstream services must be deployed with compatibility code before a database type change

PERFORMANCE_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before performance work can be meaningfully attempted

blocks.condition: A specific, measurable goal must exist before optimisation work can be scoped or evaluated
examples: a p99 latency target must be set before profiling begins
a throughput target must be agreed before a concurrency change is designed
a memory budget must be defined before a memory optimisation is measured

blocks.condition: Current performance must be measured under representative conditions before any changes are made
examples: a load test baseline must be captured before optimisation work starts
current query execution times must be recorded before indexes are added
current memory usage under load must be measured before a memory fix is evaluated

blocks.condition: A profile of where time or memory is spent must be collected before the correct optimisation is chosen
examples: a CPU profile must be taken before a hotspot is identified
a heap dump must be captured before a memory leak is traced
a query plan must be examined before an index is added

blocks.condition: Performance testing must use data and load patterns similar to production before results are trusted
examples: a copy of production data must be used before a query optimisation is validated
a representative request distribution must be simulated before a load test is meaningful
production-scale row counts must exist before index selectivity is evaluated

blocks.condition: The system must be stable with no simultaneous changes before performance measurements are attributed to a specific change
examples: no other deploys must be in progress before a before-and-after performance comparison
the environment must be stable before a benchmark is run
no unrelated feature flags must be toggled before a performance regression is confirmed

REVIEW_APPROVAL.description: What must be true before code can be accepted, merged, or released

blocks.condition: The author must have reviewed their own changes for obvious issues before requesting peer review
examples: the developer must have read the diff before opening a PR
the author must have run the tests locally before requesting review
the author must have removed debug output before requesting review

blocks.condition: A clear explanation of what changed and why must be present before reviewers are asked to review
examples: a PR description explaining the approach must exist before review is requested
links to the relevant ticket must be included before review
testing instructions must be written before a reviewer approves the PR

blocks.condition: All blocking review comments must be resolved before a PR is merged
examples: all reviewer comments marked as blocking must be replied to before merge
change requests must be resolved and re-requested for review before merge
unresolved discussions must be explicitly closed or deferred before merge

blocks.condition: All required code owners and stakeholders must have given approval before the merge is permitted
examples: the CODEOWNERS-designated reviewer must approve before merge
security-sensitive files must have a security review approval
the tech lead must approve an architectural change before it merges

blocks.condition: Every automated check registered as required on the branch must be passing before the merge button is enabled
examples: all CI checks must be green before merge is allowed
the required status check must be satisfied before the branch protection allows merge
code coverage threshold must be met before merge is permitted

RELEASE_READINESS.description: What must be true before a version of software is released

blocks.condition: A record of what changed must be written before a release is cut and announced
examples: CHANGELOG.md must be updated before a release tag is created
breaking changes must be documented before a major version is published
a release summary must be written before the GitHub release is created

blocks.condition: The version number must be incremented according to semantic versioning rules before publishing
examples: the patch version must be bumped for a bug fix release
the minor version must be bumped before a backwards-compatible feature release
the major version must be bumped before a breaking change is shipped

blocks.condition: A minimal set of critical-path tests must pass in the release environment before traffic is shifted
examples: a smoke test checking the health endpoint must pass before a prod deploy proceeds
critical user flows must be verified before a canary deploy is widened
a post-deploy check must succeed before old instances are terminated

blocks.condition: The engineer on call must know about a significant release before it goes live so they can monitor and respond
examples: the on-call engineer must be notified before a large deploy
a brief on expected behavioural changes must be shared before a release
runbook updates must be shared before a deploy that changes system behaviour

blocks.condition: Relevant internal and external parties must be informed before a release that affects them
examples: customers must be notified of breaking changes before they are deployed
the support team must be briefed before a UI change goes live
downstream API consumers must be warned before a schema change

UMG_CODING_NEEDS.description: Prerequisites specific to implementing UMG framework constructs in code

blocks.condition: A code representation of the MOLT block structure must exist before a compiler can ingest or process blocks
examples: a Block struct or class with id, type, content, and state fields must exist before ingestion is coded
a MOLT type enum must be defined before type-based authority sorting is implemented
a NeoBlock structure must be defined before the merge stage can produce one

blocks.condition: Code that can read a sleeve definition and expand it into stacks and blocks must exist before the compiler pipeline runs
examples: a JSON sleeve parser must be written before the ingestion stage is tested
module expansion logic must be implemented before the normalization stage runs
sleeve validation must be coded before an invalid sleeve can be rejected with a clear error

blocks.condition: The MOLT type authority hierarchy must be encoded in the compiler before conflict resolution can work correctly
examples: the MOLT type ordering array must be defined before blocks are sorted by authority
the comparison function for MOLT types must be written before the resolution stage
governance block priority must be hardcoded above all other types before the governance pass is trusted

blocks.condition: Code that records decisions to the Decision Trace must exist before the compiler is considered audit-compliant
examples: a TraceEvent struct must be defined before any stage can emit a trace entry
the trace writer must be wired into the governance pass before governance compliance is verified
the full trace must be serialisable before it is passed to an auditor or test

blocks.condition: A test suite that validates compiler behaviour against the UMG canon must exist before a compiler implementation is considered correct
examples: determinism tests must be available before a compiler claims canon compliance
governance supremacy tests must exist before the governance pass is considered correct
trace completeness tests must be defined before a trace implementation is verified as audit-grade
